quick_facts.label: Credential and regulator
quick_facts.value: Diploma in Bakery & Confectionery is listed by the National Council for Hotel Management and Catering Technology (NCHMCT), an autonomous body under India’s Ministry of Tourism, among its structured courses.

quick_facts.label: Entry qualification
quick_facts.value: NCHMCT’s 2026 eligibility list specifies a pass in the 10+2 Senior Secondary system, or equivalent, for the Diploma in Bakery & Confectionery.

quick_facts.label: Length
quick_facts.value: The NCHMCT course page describes the diploma as three semesters (1.5 years), comprising academic teaching followed by 24 weeks of hotel industrial training. This supersedes the node description’s “one-year” shorthand for the NCHMCT pathway.

quick_facts.label: Admissions route
quick_facts.value: For this trade diploma, NCHMCT says applications are invited and selections made by individual affiliated Institutes of Hotel Management (IHMs). NCHM JEE is the entrance examination for the separate B.Sc. in Hospitality & Hotel Administration programme, not the stated centralized admission route for this diploma.

quick_facts.label: Where it is offered
quick_facts.value: The Ministry of Tourism’s India Tourism Data Compendium 2024 reports that the diploma was offered in 2023–24 at multiple Central and State IHMs, Food Craft Institutes and selected private institutes; availability must be confirmed with the individual institute for the intended session.

sections.heading: Eligibility: complete Class 12 or equivalent
paragraphs: For the NCHMCT Diploma in Bakery & Confectionery, the current published baseline is a pass in the 10+2 Senior Secondary examination or an equivalent qualification. Applicants should still read the admission notice of the chosen IHM, because the admitting institute administers the application and may specify documentation, category/reservation requirements, medical fitness or other local conditions.

The council’s older course-scheme document additionally describes English as a subject at the Senior Secondary level. The more recent NCHMCT eligibility list for 2026 states 10+2/equivalent for this particular diploma without repeating that English condition. Where a current institute notice differs or adds detail, applicants should follow that notice and obtain clarification from the institute before applying.
bullets: Baseline published qualification: pass in 10+2 Senior Secondary or equivalent.
Check the chosen institute’s current notice for its application documents and any institute-specific conditions.
Do not assume that eligibility for the NCHMCT B.Sc. programme or NCHM JEE is identical to this diploma’s admission process.

sections.heading: Duration: 1.5 years, including industrial training
paragraphs: The official NCHMCT academic page presents the programme as three semesters (1.5 years). Its calendar breaks this into two teaching/examination terms and a third term of 24 weeks of industrial training in a hotel.

The council’s course-scheme PDF describes the same overall model in slightly different terms: one academic year of effective teaching followed by six months in industry. Therefore, applicants should plan for approximately 18 months from enrolment through completion of the prescribed industrial training, rather than treating it as a one-year qualification.
bullets: Semester I: teaching, winter break, further teaching and mid-term examinations.
Semester II: teaching, practical/preparatory period and end-term examination.
Semester III: 24 weeks of hotel industrial training.

sections.heading: Curriculum: bakery and confectionery practice with food-safety and costing foundations
paragraphs: The NCHMCT-published teaching and examination scheme identifies theory in Bakery, Confectionery, Commodities, Hygiene & Sanitation, and Food Costing. Practical work is specifically allocated to Bakery and Confectionery, alongside computer awareness and library work.

This is a skills-oriented hospitality diploma: NCHMCT says affiliated institutes provide hands-on training in institute kitchens and laboratories, followed by industrial training. The precise recipes, equipment exposure, assessment schedule and any revised syllabus should be confirmed from the admitting institute because the current online course page notes that detailed curriculum content is to be updated.
bullets: Theory areas: bakery, confectionery, commodities, hygiene and sanitation, and food costing.
Learning setting: institute kitchens/laboratories plus prescribed hotel industrial training.

sections.heading: Admission: apply to the individual affiliated institute
paragraphs: NCHMCT states that individual affiliated Institutes of Hotel Management invite applications and select candidates through their own transparent selection processes. Its general course page historically describes local advertisements around June, but this should not be treated as a fixed annual deadline; applicants should monitor the intended institute’s current notice, seat matrix and fee notice.

Although “NCHM JEE” appears in this node’s parent context, NTA describes NCHM JEE as the route for the three-year B.Sc. in Hospitality & Hotel Administration at affiliated institutes. It should not be presumed to be the entrance test for the Diploma in Bakery & Confectionery unless an individual institute’s current notice explicitly says so.
bullets: Choose an NCHMCT-affiliated institute that currently lists the diploma.
Use the institute’s own admission notice, application method, dates and selection rules.
Verify seats and whether the programme is running in the intended academic session before paying an application or admission fee.

sections.heading: Next options: build operational experience or pursue the separate hospitality degree route
paragraphs: On completion, the diploma’s prescribed hotel industrial training and hands-on bakery/confectionery preparation can support entry into bakery and pastry-oriented operational work in hospitality settings. Specific job title, pay, employer requirements and placement outcomes vary by employer, location, skill level and institute; they should not be inferred as guaranteed.

A learner who wants to move into the broader B.Sc. in Hospitality & Hotel Administration can apply through that programme’s own requirements. NCHMCT and NTA state that admission to the B.Sc. is through NCHM JEE, with 10+2/equivalent and English as an eligibility condition. The diploma is not described by these sources as an automatic lateral-entry or automatic-progression route into the B.Sc.
bullets: Use the industrial-training period to develop a portfolio of practical bakery and confectionery work and employer references.
For the B.Sc. pathway, separately meet the B.Sc. eligibility criteria and take NCHM JEE.
Ask the target B.Sc. institute directly about recognition of prior diploma learning; do not assume credit transfer or lateral entry.
